name: Murcia (Spain)

colors.symbolism: the crimson field of the historic banner of the Kingdom of Murcia
the four castles and the seven crowns

description: A crimson field bearing four golden castles arranged in two rows in the upper hoist canton, and seven golden royal crowns arranged in the lower fly.
history: The historic banner of the Kingdom of Murcia bore the four castles (granted by Alfonso X for the city's loyalty in the 13th century); successive crowns were added over the centuries to commemorate further royal honours. The flag was confirmed in the 1982 Statute of Autonomy of the Region of Murcia and Law 4/1983, with the design fixed at four castles and seven crowns.

features.symbolism: the crimson field of the historic banner of the Kingdom of Murcia, traditionally identified with the colours of Castile.

features.role: four golden castles in the upper hoist quarter
features.symbolism: the four times the city of Murcia repelled sieges and remained loyal to the Crown of Castile during the late medieval period.

features.role: seven golden royal crowns in the lower fly
features.symbolism: honours granted by successive Castilian and Spanish monarchs for the kingdom's loyalty: Alfonso X 'the Wise', Sancho IV, Pedro I, Juan II, Enrique IV, the Catholic Monarchs, and (in some accounts) Alfonso XIII.
